steps.title: Identify what “superior” means for recruitment techniques
steps.content: To support the conclusion, we need evidence that snowball sampling works better than purposive sampling at the core task: successfully recruiting participants in rural areas.

steps.title: Find which finding directly proves superiority
steps.content: Choice A demonstrates snowball sampling recruited significantly more participants with the desired characteristics than purposive sampling—the exact head-to-head comparison proving one technique's advantage over the other. The other findings measure different issues: past research participation (C), compare snowball to a random baseline rather than the purposive alternative (B), or highlight potential weaknesses in sample consistency (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
